LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC., APPLIED UNDERWRITERS CAPTIVE RISK ASSURANCE, CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANY, CONTINENTAL INDEMINTY COMPANY, APPLIED RISK SERVICES, INC., MOTION TO STRIKE CERTAIN PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT ARE SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ALLEGE THAT THE RPA IS UNLAWFUL UNDER THE RATE PROVISIONS OF THE INSURANCE CODE GOVERNING WORKERS COMPENSATION POLICIES BEGINNING AT INSURANCE CODE 11730. NOR DO PLAINTIFFS ASSERT SUCH A POSITION IN THEIR OPPOSITION MEMORANDUM. RATHER, AS THEY DID IN THEIR OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STAY BASED ON INCONVENIENT FORUM, PLAINTIFFS STATE IN OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION THAT THEIR CONTENTION THAT THE RPA IS UNLAWFUL AND VOID IS BASED ON A SEPARATE PROVISION OF THE INSURANCE CODE, SECTION 11658, DEALING WITH POLICIES AND ENDORSEMENTS, NOT RATES. JUDGE SHUBB'S DECISION IN SHASTA LINEN SUPPLY, INC. V. APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC. (E.D. CA 2016) 2016 WL 6094446 *4 IS NOT HELPFUL TO DEFENDANTS' POSITION ON THIS MOTION. JUDGE SHUBB EXPLICITLY STATED THAT HIS DECISION DID NOT BAR THE ARGUMENT THAT THE RPA WAS "ILLEGAL ON GROUNDS OTHER THAN VIOLATION OF SECTION 11735," ONE OF THE RATE STATUTES. IN FACT, RECOGNIZING THAT HIS DECISION DID NOT AFFECT AN ARGUMENT THAT THE RPA VIOLATED SECTION 11658, JUDGE SHUBB STATED THAT "IF PLAINTIFF SUCCESSFULLY ARGUES THAT THE RPA VIOLATED SECTION 11658THE COURT MAY STILL RULE THAT THE RPA WAS VOID." ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

PLAINTIFF WARWICK AMUSEMENTS CORPORATION,, WARWICK CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, WARWICK DENVER CORPORATION,, WSF BEVERAGE CORPORATION,, WARWICK MELROSE DALLAS CORPORATION, SILVER AUTUMN HOTEL (N.Y.) CORPORATION, LTD.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Plaintiffs' motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The motion is not a proper motion for judgment on the pleadings. To the extent the motion seeks a ruling on the permissible scope of discovery, plaintiffs should file a motion for protective order or assert objections to discovery served on them. In either event, it is not possible to intelligently resolve such a motion outside the context of specific discovery requests that either have been served or threatened to be served. To the extent that the motion seeks a ruling on the permissible scope of trial evidence, plaintiffs should file a motion in limine to be heard by the trial judge.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry J. Lichtenegger, Attorney at Law, Tel. 831.626.2801, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Travis R. Wall, of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP, Tel. 415.362.6000, for defendants Applied Underwriters, Inc., et al.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC., APPLIED UNDERWRITERS CAPTIVE RISK ASSURANCE, CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANY, CONTINENTAL INDEMINTY COMPANY, APPLIED RISK SERVICES, INC., MOTION TO STRIKE Certain Portions of the first amended complaint that are sought to be stricken allege that the RPA is unlawful under the rate provisions of the Insurance Code governing workers compensation policies beginning at Insurance Code 11730. Nor do plaintiffs assert such a position in their opposition memorandum. Rather, as they did in their opposition to defendants' motion to stay based on inconvenient forum, plaintiffs state in opposition to this motion that their contention that the RPA is unlawful and void is based on a separate provision of the Insurance Code, section 11658, dealing with policies and endorsements, not rates. Judge Shubb's decision in Shasta Linen Supply, Inc. v. Applied Underwriters, Inc. (E.D. CA 2016) 2016 WL 6094446 *4 is not helpful to defendants' position on this motion. Judge Shubb explicitly stated that his decision did not bar the argument that the RPA was "illegal on grounds other than violation of section 11735," one of the rate statutes. In fact, recognizing that his decision did not affect an argument that the RPA violated section 11658, Judge Shubb stated that "If plaintiff successfully argues that the RPA violated section 11658 ' the court may still rule that the RPA was void." Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Attorney Larry J. Lichtenegger, Esq. (831.626.2801) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS APPLIED UNDERWRITERS, INC., APPLIED UNDERWRITERS CAPTIVE RISK ASSURANCE, CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANY, CONTINENTAL INDEMINTY COMPANY, APPLIED RISK SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Stay Based On Inconvenient Forum is GRANTED as to all plaintiffs except Warwick California Corporation and WSF Beverage Corporation and denied as to those two plaintiffs. As to all plaintiffs except Warwick California Corporation and WSF Beverage Corporation, there is an adequate forum for the claims of those plaintiffs (either in the states their workers are located or in Nebraska), the laws of those other states apply, California has no interest in the issues raised by those plaintiffs and the other states have a strong interest in those issues. As to Warwick California Corporation and WSF Beverage Corporation, California law applies and application of California law renders both the entire RPA and the forum selection clause therein unenforceable due to AUCRA's non-compliance with Insurance Code 11658. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
